data.title: Bitcoin spot ETF had a total net outflow of US$365 million yesterday, and the ETF net asset ratio reached 5.88%
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (February 20, Eastern Time), the total net outflow of Bitcoin spot ETFs was US$365 million. Yesterday, Grayscale ETF GBTC had a single-day net outflow of US$33.525 million, and the current historical net outflow of GBTC is US$22.046 billion. Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust ETF BTC had a single-day net outflow of US$61.1029 million, and the current historical total net inflow of Grayscale Bitcoin Mini Trust BTC is US$1.199 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow yesterday was Bitwise ETF BITB, with a single-day net inflow of US$24.1428 million, and the current historical total net inflow of BITB reached US$2.187 billion. The second is VanEck ETF HODL, with a single-day net inflow of $4.1813 million. The current total net inflow of HODL has reached $867 million. 
As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETF is $114.862 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market value to Bitcoin's total market value) is 5.88%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $39.618 billion.

data.title: Hong Kong Lawmakers Explore Web3 And Virtual Assets At Consensus Conference
data.description: According to PANews, members of the Hong Kong Legislative Council's Subcommittee on Web3 and Virtual Asset Development recently attended the Consensus Conference. The delegation, including Subcommittee Chairman Ng Kit Chuang and members Ma Fung Kwok and Kan Wai Man, visited the international event focused on cryptocurrency, blockchain, and Web3 technologies held in Hong Kong. During the visit, lawmakers engaged with conference representatives to discuss the advancement of the Web3 ecosystem, the development of the cryptocurrency economy, and the expansion of business opportunities.

Earlier news, Lumoz Chain mainnet was launched, and decentralized AI products started grayscale testing.

data.title: Story updates its technology roadmap and will soon launch new features such as IP portal, authentication service, IPkit, etc.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Story, a programmable IP public chain, has released its latest roadmap. It is expected to launch an IP portal and certification services in Q2 to simplify IP on-chain. In Q3, it will upgrade the main network and release IPKit to support developers in integrating IP functions. At the same time, Story is working with Stanford FDCI to explore cutting-edge technologies such as performance optimization and on-chain storage to promote the blockchainization of intellectual property.

data.title: Yesterday, the U.S. Ethereum spot ETF had a net outflow of $13.1 million
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to Farside Investors monitoring, the US Ethereum spot ETF had a net outflow of US$13.1 million yesterday.

data.title: Hong Kong Securities Chief Highlights Global Regulatory Challenges in Crypto
data.description:
According to PANews, the Chief Executive Officer of the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission, Leung Fung-yee, addressed the Vietnam State Securities Commission seminar, highlighting the significant challenges posed by global regulatory differences on the liquidity and compliance of crypto assets. She noted that the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission recently released a virtual asset roadmap titled 'ASPIRe,' which outlines the polarized global crypto landscape with varying regulatory intensities, ranging from light oversight to strict scrutiny and prohibition.

Leung pointed out that while European jurisdictions are implementing unified standards, the United States, despite leading in virtual asset exchange-traded products and derivatives, faces issues of regulatory fragmentation. This situation underscores the role of the International Organization of Securities Commissions in setting standards.

She emphasized that regulation should be seen as a friend to innovation, not an adversary, as it helps create an environment that allows innovators to grow responsibly. This approach supports the sustainable development of the technological ecosystem.

data.title: Bitcoin's Long-Term Outlook Remains Positive Amid Institutional Adoption
data.description:
According to Odaily, despite the stagnation in Bitcoin prices, optimism about its long-term prospects persists. The potential for Bitcoin to surpass $100,000 is seen as the beginning of widespread institutional adoption, a trend that could last 10 to 20 years. Samson Mow, CEO of Bitcoin wallet company Jan3 and founder of Pixelmatic, noted that previous bull markets were subdued due to exchange backlog issues. However, with the introduction of exchange-traded funds (ETFs), traditional financial capital now faces fewer barriers to entering the Bitcoin market. Currently, capital inflow is not fully realized, as institutions are just beginning to explore the cryptocurrency market.

In 2023, BlackRock led a campaign for U.S. approval of a spot Bitcoin ETF, resulting in the launch of several Bitcoin funds in January 2024. These funds quickly became some of the fastest-growing ETFs in history. By November of last year, U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs surpassed $100 billion in net assets, with BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) leading the way at $60 billion, holding nearly 600,000 Bitcoins.
